title: Rewrite repository layer to use $PocketBase RequestPolicy.cacheAndNetwork
description:
All ~20 repository classes in packages/federfall_data/lib/src/repositories/ need to be reimplemented on top of $PocketBase instead of PbRepository.

Pattern for each repository:
- Keep Repository<T> interface unchanged
- Implement methods using $PocketBase.collection(name).{getOne,getList,create,update,delete}()
- Use requestPolicy: RequestPolicy.cacheAndNetwork (the offline-first default)
- Convert RecordModel → domain model via existing Case.fromRecord / Animal.fromRecord etc.
- Drop PrefsRecordCache usage entirely

Priority order: cases, animals, case_conditions, treatments, weights (the core daily-use data) first; then worklist_entries, findings, exams, and remaining collections.

title: Export PocketBase schema and add pb_schema.json as a bundled Flutter asset
description:

- Export the current schema from the running PocketBase instance

- Add assets/pb_schema.json to flutter: assets: in pubspec.yaml
- Wire into app bootstrap: await client.db.setSchema(schema)
- Add schema re-export step to the migration/runbook docs

NOTE: The schema must be re-exported after every PocketBase collection migration. Document this as part of the deployment runbook.

title: Shared case not visible to recipient until full page reload
description:
Sharing a case with another user does not update the recipient's app live; they must hard-reload the whole web page before the shared case becomes accessible. No realtime push arrives, and closing/reopening the case view doesn't help.

ROOT CAUSE
The live-sync (core/realtime/live_refresh.dart + collection_events.dart) only re-fetches when a PB realtime event fires on a *subscribed* collection, and PB only emits an event for the collection whose record actually changed. Sharing creates a case_shares row — the 'cases' record itself is untouched — so the recipient's subscriptions never fire:
  - cases_screen.dart watches ['cases','animals'] only (not 'case_shares').
  - case_detail_screen.dart calls no liveRefresh at all.
A full reload works because the repo fetches server-first when online and the cases list rule now matches via case_shares_via_case.shared_with.

FIX
Subscribe the case-listing surfaces to 'case_shares' too, so a share/unshare to the current user invalidates and refetches:
  - cases_screen: liveRefresh(['cases','animals','case_shares'], invalidate casesBrowserDataProvider)
  - case_detail_screen: add liveRefresh(['cases','case_shares'], invalidate caseByIdProvider(caseId)) — also covers live revoke/unshare.
  - dashboard_screen: add 'case_shares' to its ['cases','dispositions'] watch for consistency.
PB delivers case_shares events to the recipient because the case_shares view rule allows shared_with = self.
close_reason: Fixed: watch 'case_shares' on cases browser, case-detail live-sync, and dashboard KPIs so a share/revoke refetches live. flutter analyze clean. Pending live two-user verification.

title: Riverpod disposal race in async realtime/connectivity/auth providers
description: Async/async* providers crashed with 'Cannot use the Ref after it has been disposed' when disposed mid-build (during a network-length await), under hot-reload/navigation. Fixed in collection_events, connectivity (onlineStatus), auth_status, current_user, and the new fileToken provider via the ref.mounted guard pattern (move sync ref.watch before awaits; inline-teardown + return if !ref.mounted before onDispose/keepAlive). See memory riverpod-async-provider-disposal-race.
close_reason: Fixed via ref.mounted guard pattern across collection_events, connectivity, auth_status, current_user, and fileToken. Verified: flutter analyze clean, 238 app tests pass.

title: FED-3.5 — Stand up a self-hostable instance end-to-end (M1)
description: Validate the walking skeleton on ONE running instance, using the same stack any operator would. Stack is a SINGLE version-pinned PocketBase container: PB serves the API + Admin AND the Flutter web SPA from pb_public with an SPA-fallback route (see pocketbase discussions/4810) — no separate nginx/web container. Image is self-contained: pb_migrations, pb_hooks, and the built Flutter web baked in; automigrate off. Ship changes by rebuild+recreate. TLS is out of scope for the stack — operator brings their own reverse proxy; stack exposes plain HTTP on a host port. Reproducible via 'docker compose up -d'. Plus one mobile debug build for the end-to-end check. NOTE: backups/restore, monitoring, geocoding policy and the full self-host guide are Phase 8, not here. (Was: Caddy + Litestream sidecar — both dropped; stack is now nginx-free single-container.)

title: Promote quarantine into the case timeline (default 14-day initial quarantine)
description:
Today quarantine is a single `quarantine_until` date field on the `cases` record
(migration `1700000004_cases.js:101`), defaulted to `admitted_at + 14 days` by the
create hook (`pb_hooks/main.pb.js:48-52`), shown as a static Overview detail row
(`case_detail_screen.dart:507`) and edited inline in the intake edit sheet
(`edit_case_intake_sheet.dart:280-298`).

Promote quarantine into the unified case chronology so it behaves like every other
clinical record (weight, condition, medication, journal, marking, placement,
disposition). Quarantine is expected to occur/change often (extensions, early lifts),
so a single mutable field loses that history — a timeline of quarantine events records
*when* and *to what date* quarantine was set or extended.

This mirrors the "initial weight record" pattern: at intake the entered weight becomes
a real `weights` record rather than a field on the case
(`new_case_screen.dart:274-287`). Quarantine should get the same treatment — a default
**initial 14-day quarantine** created as a timeline record at case creation.

## Goal

- New quarantine timeline kind following the established pattern (see CLAUDE.md "Case
  timeline pattern"): a collection + model + repo + `@riverpod` provider + `showXSheet()`
  bottom sheet (create/edit) + a `TimelineItem` tile + a sealed `_Event` subclass in
  `case_timeline.dart`.
- Each record carries the quarantine end date (`quarantine_until`) and an event timestamp
  (when it was set/extended); an optional reason/note. Multiple records = extensions /
  re-quarantine; the latest record's end date is the current quarantine status.
- Default initial quarantine of **14 days** from admission, created automatically — like
  the initial weight record.

## Open decision (resolve before implementing)

The worklist and dashboard currently sort/surface cases by `case.quarantine_until`
(`worklist.dart`, `dashboard_providers.dart`). Two options:

1. **Keep a denormalized `quarantine_until` mirror on the case**, maintained by a hook
   to always equal the latest quarantine record (parallels how case `status` /
   animal `lifetime_status` are hook-owned). Cheapest for worklist/dashboard; no extra
   joins. **(recommended)**
2. **Drop the field entirely** and have worklist/dashboard query the quarantine records.
   Cleaner data model but more query work and N+1 risk on list views.

Decide which before touching the backend; option 1 keeps worklist/dashboard untouched.

## Where the default is created

Match the initial-weight approach: prefer creating the default quarantine record in the
backend create hook (`main.pb.js`) so it exists regardless of client, OR in
`new_case_screen.dart` alongside the intake weight. Note `main.pb.js` already owns the
14-day quarantine default — repoint it to create a record instead of (or in addition to,
if option 1) setting the field.

## Likely touch points

Backend:
- New migration: `quarantine_records` collection (relation `case` cascade, `quarantine_until`
  date required, `reason` text optional, `set_by` user, `org`, timestamps) + access rules
  (`1700000010_access_rules.js`, org-scoped).
- `main.pb.js`: create the default 14-day quarantine record on case create; (option 1) keep
  `quarantine_until` mirror in sync.
- Rule/hook tests (`tests/test_rules.py`).

Models / data:
- `federfall_models`: add `Quarantine` model + `fromRecord` mapper (see `Disposition`).
- `federfall_data`: `QuarantineRecordsRepository` + provider wiring in
  `repository_providers.dart`.

App:
- `features/cases/quarantine/`: providers, `quarantine_sheet.dart`, `quarantine_tile.dart`.
- `case_timeline.dart`: `_QuarantineEvent` subclass + fetch in build.
- `add_entry_sheet.dart`: add quarantine to the "add" picker.
- `case_detail_screen.dart`: drop the static Overview quarantine row (now on History).
- `edit_case_intake_sheet.dart`: remove the inline quarantine field.
- l10n: `app_de.arb` + `app_en.arb` strings (German primary).

Don't forget codegen (`build_runner` for freezed/riverpod, `flutter gen-l10n`) and the
quality gates (`flutter analyze` + tests; package `dart analyze`/`dart test`).
design:
DECISION (resolved): use a PocketBase VIEW collection for the worklist/dashboard quarantine end date — NOT a denormalized mirror field and NOT a client-side join. This is the established pattern here (see medication_due_view 1700000024 — same shape: compute a derived date per record so the worklist gets it in one query). 'Current quarantine end per case' = MAX(quarantine_until) over the case's quarantine_records (or latest by event timestamp if early-lift records are allowed — decide alongside lift semantics).

Two shapes: (1) dedicated view 'quarantine_current' (id=case, quarantine_until=MAX(...)), org-scoped like medication_due; or (2) cheaper — add a computed quarantine_until column to the existing case_summaries view so current readers get it for free.

Realtime caveat is already solved: views emit no realtime, so add 'quarantine_records' to worklistLiveCollections (worklist_providers.dart) — the base-collection watch + 1-minute worklistTicker already handle the clock-driven 'quarantine ending' transition (the ticker comment literally names it). This means NO mirror field and NO sync hook: the only hook work is creating the default 14-day quarantine record at case create. Supersedes the option-1/option-2 'Open decision' in the description.
acceptance_criteria: Quarantine appears as a timeline kind in the case History (provider + showXSheet + TimelineItem tile + _Event subclass); a default 14-day quarantine record is created automatically at case creation (like the initial weight record); the static Overview quarantine row and the inline edit-sheet field are removed; worklist/dashboard quarantine sorting still works; flutter analyze clean + tests green; backend migration + hook + rule tests in place.

title: Add background sync via WorkManager (iOS/Android) — push writes when app is backgrounded
description:
pocketbase_drift's sync retry only fires when the app is active and connectivity returns. For wildlife rehabilitators who log a medication and immediately put the phone in their pocket, the write queues locally but only syncs when they re-open the app.

- Evaluate adding workmanager (^3.0.0) to trigger sync on backgrounded iOS/Android
- On connectivity RESTORED event, schedule a WorkManager one-off task that calls _retrySyncForAllServices()
- On web: use ServiceWorker background sync API (navigator.serviceWorker.ready.then(...))
- Add retryLocal() progress reporting to the UI (sync indicator in app bar)
- Test: kill app immediately after creating a case offline → app restart should sync it

title: Handle logout — set clearOnLogout: false on $AuthStore; verify schema persistence
description:
pocketbase_drift's $AuthStore.clear() wipes the entire local Drift DB by default. For Federfall, the cache should be preserved on logout (carers may share a device), only cleared on explicit app reset.

- Set clearOnLogout: false on $AuthStore
- Verify schema (pb_schema.json) is NOT cleared on logout
- Verify case/case_condition/treatment data IS cleared on logout
- Test: log in → create a case offline → log out → log back in → case should be gone
- Remove SecureAuthTokenStorage / PrefsAuthTokenStorage once $AuthStore.prefs is wired

title: Adapt LiveRefresh / case_realtime.dart for pocketbase_drift's realtime bridge
description:
pocketbase_drift pipes PocketBase realtime events into the local Drift DB (onEvent() → $create / $update / $delete on local SQLite). Federfall's existing ServiceMixin, LiveRefresh and case_realtime.dart wiring should be checked for compatibility.

- Verify ServiceMixin's watchRecords() stream still fires and triggers UI rebuilds
- Verify onEvent() writes to Drift do not interfere with existing stream logic
- Adapt caseRealtime to use $PocketBase.realtime if needed
- Test: create/update/delete a case from Admin UI → verify Flutter app updates within 5s

Note: the realtime service is currently delegated to the base PocketBase SDK class (not overridden in $PocketBase).

title: Set up WASM SQLite for web (sqlite3.wasm + drift_worker.dart.js)
description:
pocketbase_drift uses Drift with SQLite. On web this requires WASM files served from the web/ directory.

- Download sqlite3.wasm from the sqlite3.dart releases
- Download drift_worker.dart.js from the drift package
- Place both in apps/federfall/web/
- Verify web build succeeds (flutter build web)
- Test offline reads/writes in browser (Chrome DevTools → Network → Offline)

Currently Federfall uses SharedPreferences (localStorage) for web cache — this replaces it with a proper SQLite store.

title: Migrate to pocketbase_drift — offline-first sync engine
description:
Replace the current read-through cache (PrefsRecordCache) with pocketbase_drift (^0.4.3), a proper offline-first SQLite-backed layer for PocketBase.

CURRENT BEHAVIOUR (broken):
- All writes (create/update/delete) throw RepositoryException.network immediately when offline
- Cache is bounded: 100 records / 20 lists per collection, no TTL, FIFO eviction
- offlineBanner shows "Offline – showing saved data" — misleading, because no writes are possible
- No background sync; writes never survive app restart

DESIRED BEHAVIOUR:
- Writes queue locally in SQLite when offline; sync automatically on reconnect
- Reads served from cache instantly; network updates in background
- Conflict resolution: 5 retries then silent discard (acceptable for wildlife rehab use case)
- Schema-bundled validation for local writes

See: https://pub.dev/packages/pocketbase_drift
See: internal code review — pocketbase_drift gap analysis

title: Sheets/dialogs lack PopScope for unsaved-changes / back-nav
description:
Sheets and dialogs don't use PopScope (or PopEntry) to control back navigation on Android. An accidental swipe or back gesture can dismiss a form with unsaved changes without confirmation.

Recommendation: wrap sheet/dialog content in PopScope to intercept dismissal when the form is dirty and prompt to confirm discarding unsaved changes.

Source: code review finding #2.

description: On wide/expanded screens (two-pane list+detail layout, federfall-zbe), the case detail pane appears to show a 'not found' state instead of the empty/select-a-case placeholder when the signed-in user has no cases yet (e.g. a fresh account). title: OAuth2 self-service provisioning: guest role + group gating + first-user supervisor
description: Make OAuth2 usable as a sign-in method that can also self-provision accounts, on top of federfall-uvf (which enabled OAuth2 + env provider config). Decided shape: HYBRID. (1) Add a 'guest' role; new self-registered OAuth2 users default to guest. (2) Guest-proof the access rules: a guest is is_active=true (can log in) but the access boundary excludes role='guest' everywhere, so they see/do nothing until promoted — show an 'awaiting access' state. (3) pb_hooks OAuth2 provisioning (onRecordAuthWithOAuth2Request, isNewRecord): read IdP groups from oAuth2User.rawUser; optional group->role mapping (env), optional allow-list gating (env); set role/org/is_active on the new record (also fixes the role-required 400 that blocks OAuth2 signup today). (4) Chicken-and-egg: the FIRST user (when no active supervisor exists) OR a configured supervisor group becomes supervisor. (5) Optional env toggle to disable password auth so OAuth2 can be the ONLY method (info endpoint already reports auth.password=false; login screen already hides the password form). NOTE: the OAuth2 login-screen buttons + awaiting-access UI ride on federfall-pj3; the provisioning hook only fires on a real OAuth2 flow so end-to-end needs a live IdP — guest-rule enforcement is testable via the API + rule tests.
notes:
Backend DONE + tested: guest role + access-rule wall-off (migration 1700000033; 6 new rule tests, suite now 98/0), OAuth2 self-provisioning hook (oauth2_provisioning.pb.js: first-user/admin-group->supervisor, group->role mapping, allowed-groups gating, sets role/org/is_active), and FEDERFALL_PASSWORD_AUTH toggle for OAuth2-only (info reports password:false). Documented in compose/DEPLOYMENT/README. CAVEAT: the provisioning hook loads cleanly and is wired to the confirmed onRecordAuthWithOAuth2Request API, but its group-mapping/role-assignment path only fires on a real OAuth2 flow — needs live-IdP verification (no mock IdP in the test rig). App UI (provider buttons, guest awaiting-access screen, redirect handling) is federfall-pj3.
VERIFIED end-to-end against the mock IdP (navikt/mock-oauth2-server): drove the full auth-code flow (authorize -> code -> /api/collections/users/auth-with-oauth2 with PKCE) — admin-group user provisions as supervisor, no-group user as guest, both with org + is_active set and signed in. Fixed 4 bugs found in testing (see commit febb4c2): createRule (oauth2 context), hook now uses e.createData not e.record, createData object assign, dropped protected verified field. Rule tests 99/0 incl. anonymous-create-denied. Backend complete.

title: Responsive/adaptive layouts for web & large screens (tablet)
description:
The app is built mobile-first and works well on phones, but on the web and larger screens (tablet, desktop browser) it just stretches the single-pane phone UI — navigating into a list item opens a whole new full-screen 'window' where a wide screen has room to show list AND detail together. Make the layout adaptive so large screens use their space.

Concrete opportunities:
- List-detail (two-pane): cases list, animals registry, aviaries — show the list on the left and the selected item's detail on the right on wide screens, instead of pushing a new route. Falls back to the current full-screen navigation on narrow screens.
- Case detail: on wide screens show Overview and History side-by-side simultaneously, rather than behind the Übersicht/Verlauf tabs. Keep the tabs on narrow screens.
- App chrome: NavigationRail (or extended rail) on wide screens vs the bottom NavigationBar on mobile (the nav shell).

Approach — derive proper UX concepts first, then implement the Flutter way, NO code duplication:
- Define breakpoints once (compact / medium / expanded, per Material 3 window-size classes) and a single place that maps them to layout decisions; don't sprinkle MediaQuery checks.
- Reuse the EXISTING composable widgets (CaseTimeline, the Overview sections, list tiles, DetailHeader, etc.) in both the single-pane and two-pane arrangements — the panes are just different containers around the same widgets, not reimplementations.
- Use Material 3 canonical layouts / adaptive scaffolding (LayoutBuilder + the official adaptive patterns, e.g. flutter_adaptive_scaffold or an equivalent in-house AdaptiveScaffold) and NavigationRail<->NavigationBar.
- Mind go_router: the selected list item / detail must stay URL-addressable (deep links, browser back) — likely a StatefulShellRoute or a selected-id query/path that drives the detail pane; the two-pane and pushed-route views should share one source of selection state.
- Keep it accessible and not regress the phone experience.

This is sizeable and design-led — start by producing the UX concept (breakpoints, which surfaces get two-pane, how selection/routing works, mockups), then split implementation per surface (it may warrant promotion to an epic with child issues once the concept is agreed).
acceptance_criteria: A documented adaptive-layout concept exists (breakpoints, per-surface behaviour, routing/selection model). On expanded widths: the cases/animals/aviaries lists show list+detail side-by-side and the case detail shows Overview+History together; on compact widths the current phone UX is unchanged. Layout decisions come from one shared breakpoint source; the same content widgets are reused across panes with no duplication; deep links and browser back still work; flutter analyze clean + tests green.

title: Gate all write-operation UI behind the user's permissions
description:
Across the app, hide (or disable) every write/mutating UI control when the current user is not allowed to perform it, so the UI matches the server access rules instead of offering actions that fail on save. The backend access rules in 1700000010_access_rules.js are the real boundary; this is about not surfacing dead-end controls (esp. to read-only shared viewers and lower-privileged roles).

Currently inconsistent: some surfaces gate (case Overview actions and the new add-entry FAB use 'active carer || supervisor'; team/admin screens use roles.dart helpers; aviary FAB uses canManage), while others don't (the per-tile edit/delete/overflow menus on every timeline tile — journal, weight, exam, condition, prescription, dose, marking, placement, follow-up, disposition — appear regardless of write permission).

Work:
- Establish ONE source of truth for 'can I write to this case' (e.g. a canEditCase(case, user) helper in core/auth/roles.dart, or a provider) and stop duplicating the inline 'activeCarer == me.id || supervisor' check (already copied in case_detail_screen _CaseActions and the add-entry FAB).
- Audit every write affordance and gate it: timeline tile edit/delete menus, the add-entry FAB (already done in xc8.1 — fold into the shared helper), case edit/status/share, sharing changes, aviary add/edit, animal edit, admin code-lists/team, profile, etc.
- Decide hide vs. disable per control (prefer hide for menus/FABs; disable+tooltip where the control must stay visible).
- Cover coordinator vs supervisor vs carer vs read-only-shared distinctions consistently (see existing roles.dart helpers).

Note: the org-scoped, private-by-default + opt-in sharing model means a user may have read-only access to a shared case — those viewers must see no write controls.
acceptance_criteria: A read-only viewer sees no write affordances (no add-entry FAB, no per-tile edit/delete menus, no edit/status/share) on cases shared read-only; permitted users see them as before; the case write check lives in one shared helper used everywhere; no UI offers an action the server will reject. Verified against the rule tests / a running stack where rules apply.
close_reason: Closed

title: Uploaded image doesn't appear until a manual refresh
description:
After adding/uploading a photo (e.g. a journal entry attachment), the image does not show immediately — it only appears after a pull-to-refresh, navigating away and back, or the next realtime tick. Looks like a race/refresh-ordering bug in the upload→display path.

Likely suspects to investigate:
- Provider refresh ordering: the create/update sheet invalidates the owning list provider (e.g. journalForCaseProvider) on save, but the new record's file field / the read-through cache may not yet reflect the just-uploaded file when the tile rebuilds.
- File token timing: protected file fields need a token from fileTokenProvider (FED-8.1); the attachment strip shows a spinner until repo+token resolve. If the token isn't ready, or the URL is built before it is, the first render can miss.
- Image cache: CachedFileImage keys by token-stripped URL (fileCacheKey, federfall-xu3). A freshly-created file id that was probed/cached as a 404/miss before the bytes existed could serve a stale negative result until evicted.
- Multipart create returns the record; confirm the attachments filename list on the returned RecordModel is used to build the URL immediately, rather than waiting for a re-fetch.

Repro: open a case → add a journal entry with a photo → save. Expected: the thumbnail appears in the new timeline tile right away. Actual: blank/absent until a manual refresh.

Touches: journal attachment strip (journal_entry_tile.dart _AttachmentStrip), the create/save + invalidate path (journal_entry_sheet.dart), fileTokenProvider, and CachedFileImage/fileCacheKey. Likely applies to other image fields too (exam/finder/clinical).
acceptance_criteria: A newly uploaded image renders in its tile immediately after save, without a manual refresh; no stale-cache miss; holds for journal attachments and any other image field that shares the path.
close_reason: Closed

title: Scope carer (Pflegestelle) visibility: only their own animals + aviaries
description:
Today the access rules (backend/pocketbase/pb_migrations/1700000010_access_rules.js) make animals, markings and aviaries org-wide readable by EVERY active member (list/view = 'org = @request.auth.org'). A 'carer' (role enum: carer|coordinator|supervisor — carer == Pflegestelle) should instead only see THEIR OWN data. Coordinators/supervisors keep the org-wide view.

REQUIREMENT
1. Aviaries: a carer sees only aviaries assigned to them. The 'aviaries' collection already has a 'keeper' relation -> users, so the carer branch is roughly: keeper = @request.auth.id (||
COORD_SUP for the wider roles). list+view rules.
2. Animals: a carer sees only 'their own' animals =
   (a) animals currently assigned to one of their aviaries — animals.current_aviary.keeper = @request.auth.id, AND
   (b) animals they have/had a case with — animals linked to a case where they are active_carer (cases_via_animal.active_carer ?= @request.auth.id). Confirm the exact back-relation name.

OPEN QUESTIONS (the '?' in the request)
- 'have (had?) a case with': active_carer is a single CURRENT field; past carers are not tracked except possibly via case_shares. Decide whether past involvement counts and, if so, how to express it (case_shares.shared_with? a carer-history?). 
- Re-identification tension: the rule comment + memory 'animals-created-via-intake-or-aviary-resident' treat animals/markings as an ORG-WIDE identity layer for re-identification/dedup. Scoping animal visibility per-carer may break carers re-identifying a returning bird. Decide: keep re-id server-side / coordinator-only, or leave markings org-wide while scoping animals?
- markings: scope alongside animals or keep org-wide for ring-number re-id?
- Does scoping animals break any current UI (animals registry, aviary detail resident list) for carers? Audit lib/features for queries that assume org-wide animals.

IMPLEMENTATION
- New migration that rewrites the animals + aviaries (and maybe markings) list/view rules with a role-gated branch: COORD_SUP -> org-wide; carer -> the scoped filter above. No schema change needed for aviaries (keeper exists) or animal-in-aviary (current_aviary exists); only a migration if 'had a case' needs new history.
- Rules are the real security boundary — add/extend cases in backend/pocketbase/tests/test_rules.py (run via tests/run.sh against a live PB) covering: carer sees own aviary + its residents + own-case animals; carer does NOT see another carer's aviary/animals; coordinator/supervisor still see all.

Relates to FED-1.11 (federfall-bhx, access rules) and Phase 8 hardening (federfall-49l).
close_reason: Decided the current org-wide animals/aviaries visibility model is fine as-is; carer-scoping not pursued.

title: Offline note shown spuriously after returning to the app
description:
After bringing the app back to the foreground, the offline note (core/connectivity/offline_banner.dart) sometimes appears even though the device is online — annoying false positive.

Likely cause in core/connectivity/connectivity.dart (onlineStatusProvider): on resume the server /health probe can transiently fail (network interface still waking up, or the probe times out) and latch OnlineStatus.offline, and re-evaluation only happens on a connectivity_plus interface change or the 30s heartbeat — so the stale 'offline' lingers. Returning from background may not emit an interface change, so nothing re-probes promptly.

Fix directions to evaluate:
- Re-probe on app lifecycle resume (AppLifecycleState.resumed), not just on interface change / heartbeat.
- Treat a single failed probe as tentative: require N consecutive failures (or a short retry) before showing offline, and re-check quickly after a failure rather than waiting for the next 30s tick.
- Avoid fl​ipping to offline during the brief resume window.

Acceptance: returning to the foreground while genuinely online does not flash the offline note; a real outage still surfaces it promptly. Touches connectivity.dart (onlineStatus) and possibly a lifecycle observer; offline_banner.dart unchanged.
close_reason: Closed

title: Timeline entries too condensed to read — give them room, especially exams
description:
The case History timeline is core functionality but each entry is rendered very condensed (shared TimelineItem + per-kind _Event tiles in case_timeline.dart; exam tiles in features/cases/exams/exam_tile.dart). Clinically important detail — especially exams (vitals + findings) — is hard to actually read at a glance.

Make entries more legible; it is fine for them to be bigger:
- Allow multi-line content (titles/notes not truncated to one line).
- Exams in particular should show their structured data readably (vitals, BCS, findings) rather than a one-line summary — consider an expanded inline layout or a richer card for exams.
- Keep the unified single-chronology pattern (one consistent TimelineItem look), just with more vertical room and clear hierarchy per kind.

Acceptance: a carer can read an exam's key values without opening it; long notes are not clipped; the timeline still reads as one consistent chronology. Touches timeline_item.dart, case_timeline.dart (the _Event tiles), and exams/exam_tile.dart.
close_reason: Closed

title: Timeline 'add entry' needs a better UX than a tiny + → giant menu
description:
Adding a timeline entry (note, weight, condition, exam, prescription, dose, marking, handoff, placement, follow-up) is core, frequent functionality, but today it is a single small PopupMenuButton (Icons.add) in the History tab header that opens a long ~11-item popup menu (case_timeline.dart:162). That is poor discoverability and a lot of scanning for the most-used actions.

Design a better add-entry UX. Options to evaluate:
- A prominent FAB (or extended FAB) on the History tab instead of a header icon.
- A bottom sheet of large, labelled, icon-led action tiles (grouped: clinical / meds / placement / admin) instead of a flat text menu.
- Surfacing the 2-3 most common kinds (e.g. note, weight, exam) as direct quick-actions, with 'more' for the rest.
- Possibly context-aware ordering (e.g. 'dose' only meaningful once a prescription exists).

Acceptance: adding the common entry kinds is fast and discoverable; the full set stays reachable; consistent with the app's unified-view preference. Touches case_timeline.dart (the PopupMenuButton + showXSheet calls) and the l10n add-action strings.
design:
DECIDED (see exploration artifact: https://claude.ai/code/artifact/1a70219d-f3f1-47bf-9ac8-f251051709fb).

Chosen design: an extended FAB '+ Eintrag' (mirroring the aviary FAB.extended pattern, permission-gated, thumb-zone, floating over both case tabs) that opens ONE adaptive grouped bottom sheet. Quick-row of frequent-on-this-case kinds on top; kinds grouped Klinisch / Medikation / Bewegung / Abschluss below; context gates honoured (Dosis needs an active Rx, Ergebnis only before disposal). The most context-bound kind (Dosis) also stays inline on its prescription tile. One consistent surface — no fragmentation.

Implemented across the child issues (phases 1-4 + inline dose). Phase 1 alone satisfies this epic's acceptance.
close_reason: Closed

title: Evaluate Protected file fields + tokens for case/animal photos
description:
PocketBase serves file fields PUBLICLY by full URL by default (the random
filename suffix is the only guard). Sensitive files can be marked **Protected**
in the field options, then fetched with a short-lived **file token**
(pb.files.getToken() → append ?token=... to the URL).

Today every image (intake_photos on cases, journal_entries.attachments,
animals.photo) uses an UNPROTECTED field — PbRepository.fileUrl() builds a
plain /api/files URL with no token (the doc-comment even notes "the file field
is unprotected, so the URL is usable without an auth token"). So anyone with
the URL can view a patient/finder photo, bypassing the org-scoped access rules
that protect the records themselves.

TASK:
1. Decide which file fields should be Protected. Clinical/finder-linked photos
   (case intake_photos, journal attachments, possibly animal photo) likely
   should be, to match the private-by-default record model. Pure-public assets
   (none currently) stay open.
2. Backend: migration to set `protected: true` on those file fields.
3. Client: fileUrl() must request and append a file token for protected fields
   (pb.files.getToken(), cache it ~the token TTL, append ?token=). Image.network
   needs the tokenised URL; the full-screen viewer + share path too. Handle
   token refresh/expiry and offline (cached images already fetched are fine;
   new fetches need a token → require connectivity).
4. Verify with backend rule tests: a protected file URL without a valid token
   is rejected; with a token from an authorized user it works; another org's
   user cannot get a usable token.

Touches: pb_migrations (protected flag), packages/federfall_data PbRepository
.fileUrl + a token helper, image_viewer + thumbnails + animal_avatar, tests.
Note: protected files are NOT thumbnail-cacheable by plain URL, and the token
is short-lived — factor that into the thumbnail/loading code.
close_reason: Protected the three clinical/finder-linked file fields (cases.intake_photos, journal_entries.attachments, animals.photo) via migration 1700000027; client appends a short-lived file token (fileTokenProvider -> pb.files.getToken(), ~90s cache) to file URLs via fileUrl(token:). Verified by backend rule tests (bare URL rejected, same-org token works, cross-org token rejected) + data-layer unit test. Thumbnail cache-churn deferred to federfall-xu3.

title: Live-sync all browse/summary screens
description: Extend Pattern A live-sync to every main browse/summary view via a reusable WidgetRef.liveRefresh(collections, onChange) primitive: dashboard summary, cases list, animals registry, aviaries, statistics, animal lifetime detail. Migrate worklistLive onto the same primitive for one consistent pattern.
close_reason: Reusable WidgetRef.liveRefresh(collections, onChange) primitive; wired into dashboard summary + Today card, cases list, animals registry, aviaries, statistics, and animal lifetime detail. Migrated worklistLive onto it (deleted the provider; collections now a public const). One consistent pattern; analyze clean, 235/235.

title: P1d — realtime live-sync (case detail + worklist)
description: Wire PocketBase realtime SSE subscriptions: a reusable mechanism that invalidates the relevant providers on collection events. Start with case-detail timeline (live teammate updates on shared cases) and the worklist. Respect access rules. Note: reverse proxy must not buffer /api/realtime.
close_reason: Realtime live-sync (Pattern A): collectionEvents SSE provider + CaseLive notifier (case detail) + worklistLive (worklist). Events only TRIGGER the existing loaders via invalidateCaseTimeline / invalidate(worklist) — one data path shared with pull-to-refresh; no hand-merged second copy. Re-subscribes on reconnect and catches up. analyze clean, 235/235.

title: P1c — consistent pull-to-refresh across list/detail screens
description: Add RefreshIndicator + provider invalidation to the screens that lack it (cases list, animals, aviaries, case detail). Match the dashboard/today/statistics pattern.
close_reason: Pull-to-refresh added to cases list, animals registry, aviaries, and case detail (both tabs). Case detail reuses a new invalidateCaseTimeline helper (kept next to CaseTimeline's source list). analyze clean, 235/235.

title: P1b — graceful offline-write messaging
description: When a write fails with a network error, surface a clear 'you're offline, not saved' message (not the generic error), keyed on RepositoryException.isNetwork + the connectivity state.
close_reason: Offline write/error copy is now clear and reassuring (offline + retry + entry kept), paired with the fail-fast writes from 2o3.1. Single mapping point (errorOffline) so it's consistent across all 41 call sites.

title: P1a — connectivity provider + offline banner
description: Add connectivity_plus; a provider that combines interface state with an /api/health reachability check into an OnlineStatus (online/offline). App-wide offline banner in the nav shell.
close_reason: connectivity_plus + onlineStatus (interface + ServerProbe /api/health) + OfflineBanner. Plus the core read-path fix: PbRepository now caps every request at networkTimeout (no more multi-minute hang on an unreachable server) and, when onlineStatus already knows we're offline, serves the cache instantly / fails writes fast. data 11/11, app 235/235, analyze clean.

title: Offline/sync Phase 1 — live-sync, connectivity UX, consistent refresh
description: Make online behavior feel well-made without full offline-first. Wire PocketBase realtime where it helps, add connectivity awareness + offline banner, make pull-to-refresh consistent across list/detail screens, and make offline writes fail gracefully. Follow-up to the FED-2.6 read cache. Phases 2 (offline write outbox) and 3 (full offline-first) deferred.
close_reason: Offline/sync Phase 1 complete: connectivity + offline banner, fail-fast offline reads/writes (timeout + known-offline short-circuit), graceful offline messaging, consistent pull-to-refresh, and realtime live-sync (case detail + worklist) via the invalidation pattern. Phases 2 (offline write outbox) and 3 (full offline-first) remain deferred.

title: Exam: add weight + temperature; route intake weight to a Weight entry; drop dead case clinical fields
design:
GOAL
Reconcile the case's clinical fields with the relational exam/weights model.
Weight + temperature + mucous membranes become exam/weights data; the dead
exam_* columns and the case-only intake_weight_g are removed. Validated against
docs/REQUIREMENTS.md (lines 162, 168) — temperature/mm are spec'd; intake weight
is a spec headline fast-path field (lines 79, 281, 341), so it STAYS on the
intake form but writes a Weight entry instead of a case column.

PRINCIPLE: every exam datum is its own typed, optional field, persisted ONLY
when the user enters it (sparse). NOTHING is folded into free-text notes.

PART A — weight on the exam
- Optional weight field in the exam sheet vitals zone. On save, if entered,
  CREATE a Weight (animal, case, weight_g, measured_at = examined_at) via
  weightsRepository. Not stored on the exam record — `weights` stays the single
  source of truth + trend. Invalidate weightsForCaseProvider.
- Create-only on the exam-CREATE path (hide/disable on edit) to avoid dup; a
  weights.exam relation for true edit-sync is a later option.

PART B — temperature + mucous membranes (new clinical vitals, all spec-listed)
- Add to the exams collection + Exam model + sheet vitals zone, each optional
  and stored only when entered:
  - `temperature` (number, nullable; °C). Birds normal ~40-42 °C.
  - `mm_color` (select: pink/pale/cyanotic/icteric/injected) — new wire enum.
  - `mm_texture` (select: moist/tacky/dry) — new wire enum.
  (Free-text optional fields are the lighter alt to the selects, if preferred.)

PART C — intake weight: KEEP the field, change what it writes
- new_case_screen keeps ONE weight input (fast-path per spec). In _create,
  after the case is created, if a weight was entered, create a Weight
  (animal, case = created.id, weight_g, measured_at = admitted_at) instead of
  writing case.intake_weight_g.
- Note: if the optional entry exam (blp.4) is then opened, leave its weight
  field blank to avoid a duplicate at the same timestamp (or prefill from the
  just-created Weight). Minor; accept or refine.
- Remove the weight field from edit_case_intake_sheet (weights are edited on
  the timeline now).

PART D — remove dead case clinical columns
- The 14 cases.exam_* columns (exam_bcs, exam_dehydration, exam_attitude,
  exam_temperature, exam_mm_color, exam_mm_texture, exam_head, exam_cns,
  exam_cardiopulmonary, exam_gi, exam_musculoskeletal, exam_integument,
  exam_forelimb, exam_hindlimb) are mapped in the Case model but never read or
  written by any UI — pure vestige of the original flat-exam FED-4.8 plan, now
  superseded by exams/exam_findings. Drop them (migration) + remove the fields
  from medical_case.dart. They are empty (no writer) → NO backfill needed.
- intake_weight_g: keep the column for old rows; add a one-off migration
  backfilling existing intake_weight_g -> Weight (animal, case,
  measured_at = admitted_at) so historic intake weights reach the trend, then
  stop writing it (PART C). Drop the column + Case.intakeWeightG in a later
  migration once nothing reads it.
- case_detail_screen.dart:361 currently shows intake_weight_g — switch to read
  the earliest/latest Weight from the trend, or drop the standalone display.

TOUCHES: exams migration (temperature/mm_color/mm_texture), exam_sheet, exam
model + enums (MmColor, MmTexture), new_case_screen (+test), edit_case_intake_
sheet (+test), case_detail_screen (+test), medical_case.dart (+mapper test),
weights provider/repo, l10n + cases_labels; drop-exam_* migration +
intake_weight_g backfill migration.
close_reason: Exam now captures weight (→Weight entry, create-path), temperature + mucous-membrane vitals (typed selects). Intake weight kept as fast-path but writes a Weight entry; removed from edit sheet + case detail. Dropped 14 dead cases.exam_* columns + Case model fields; backfill migration intake_weight_g→weights. migrate up clean, 73/73 rule tests, models 17/17, app 224/224.

title: First-run: require a VERIFIED federfall server before login, and let it inform login options
description:
On mobile/native first start the user must configure the backend before the login screen appears (the gate already does native→/setup→/login). Two gaps remain:

1. GENUINE-FEDERFALL VERIFICATION. ServerProbe today only checks GET /api/health == 200, which ANY PocketBase (or anything returning a 200 health) passes — ProbeNotFederfall never fires for a generic PB. Add a federfall identity/capabilities endpoint (pb_hooks route, e.g. GET /api/federfall/info, unauthenticated) returning a federfall marker + schema/app version + min supported client + login options. Upgrade ServerProbe to require that marker before ProbeReachable; reject generic PB / random 200s with a clear 'not a Federfall server' message (server_probe.dart already has ProbeNotFederfall).

2. SERVER INFORMS LOGIN. The verified server's capabilities should drive the login screen: which auth methods are enabled (password, OAuth providers), self-signup vs invite-only, password-reset availability, org/branding name. Expose the fetched capabilities via a provider; the router gate must ensure they resolve before /login renders (login adapts its buttons/links accordingly). Optionally surface 'update required' when min-client > current build.

Touches: backend pb_hooks (new info route), core/server/server_probe.dart, server_config_controller.dart, features/server_setup/setup_screen.dart, features/auth/login_screen.dart, router gate. Acceptance: first start forces server entry; a non-federfall URL is rejected; once a verified server is set the login screen reflects that server's enabled options; config persists across restarts; rule/probe tests cover federfall-vs-generic-PB.
close_reason: Closed

title: medication_due view + worklist single-query fetch (perf)
description: Worklist N+1: meds+doses fetched per case. Add a medication_due PB view computing next_due per active prescription (join latest administration; scheduled=last_dose+interval_hours, once=started_at if never given), org+active_carer scoped. Client: drop per-case meds/doses fetch (one view query), collapse follow-ups + animals to single filtered list() queries. buildWorklist consumes MedicationDue (next_due precomputed); next-due correctness moves to the backend rule test.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add animals without a case (aviary-only residents)
description: Today an animal identity is created only via case intake. Allow adding an animal directly — e.g. a permanent/aviary resident that never had an acute case — so it can be an aviary member without a case. Needs: a create-animal entry point (animals registry FAB and/or aviary detail 'add resident'), the animal create flow (species/sex/name/markings), and optionally placing it straight into an aviary (lifetime_status=in_aviary, current_aviary). Verify access rules allow case-less animals.
close_reason: Closed

title: Record time-of-day on doses & journal entries (worklist prereq)
description: Medication administrations and journal entries store a full DateTime but the UI only picks a date, zeroing the time. 'Next-due' med scheduling (cr3.1 worklist) needs the actual time a dose was given. Add a date+time picker (shared pickDateTime + DateField showTime) and display time on the administration & journal timeline tiles. Convert UTC->local for display/picking.
close_reason: Closed

title: Handle quarantine times (not yet implemented)
description: Quarantine is only partially handled. The FED-1.12 hook stamps a default quarantine_until (admitted_at + 14 days) on case create, and the dashboard surfaces 'quarantine ending soon', but there is no way to: set/edit the quarantine period per case (species/reason-dependent), see quarantine state clearly on the case, mark quarantine complete/lifted, or factor it into the worklist (UX Phase D). Decide the quarantine model (fixed default vs editable; what 'ending' triggers) and build the UI + any hook/schema changes. Surfaced during the UX-90 push.
close_reason: Quarantine shown on case Overview (with ended state) + editable/liftable via intake sheet (future-date picker). Worklist integration deferred to Phase D.

title: Rethink app navigation / information architecture
description: Navigation is getting convoluted as features land. The 4-tab shell (Dashboard/Cases/Animals/Aviaries) plus app-bar actions (profile, admin, stats) plus growing admin sub-routes (members, org settings, conditions) and per-screen FABs/menus lack a coherent IA. Audit the whole navigation surface and propose a cleaner structure: where admin/org functions live (one 'Admin' hub vs scattered app-bar icons), how settings/profile are reached, whether reporting belongs in the shell, and how deep actions (new case, handoff, worklist) surface. Deliverable: an IA proposal + the refactor. Cross-cutting across all UX phases.
close_reason: Closed

title: Hand case to another carer (set active_carer)
description: Action on case detail to reassign cases.active_carer to another member. The share-on-handoff hook already auto-grants the previous carer read access on change. Distinct from placements (which log physical handoff/chain-of-custody).
close_reason: Handoff/move split with clear titles + same-carer guard; handoff surfaced on actions card. Reassign stays tied to a placement record.

title: Manual case status transitions UI
description: Once the lifecycle is settled (blp.1), let carers move a case through its states (e.g. in_care -> in_treatment -> rehab -> ready_for_release) from the case detail. Today only hooks set in_care/disposed. Depends on the blp.1 decision.
close_reason: 3-state lifecycle (migration+enum) + bottom actions card (status/edit/share), carer/supervisor gated; overflow fixed

title: Edit & delete disposition (re-open case)
description: A wrong outcome is currently permanent and closes the case with no undo. Allow editing a disposition and deleting it; deleting the terminal disposition must re-open the case (status back to in_care) and revert animal lifetime_status/current_aviary. Mirror the disposition hook's side-effects on delete.
close_reason: Disposition edit/delete + reconciliation hooks (re-open case, revert animal); verified via run.sh

title: Edit animal identity (name/species/sex)
description: Animal name/species/sex are write-once today (only the avatar is editable). Add an edit sheet on the animal detail header. Updates animals via PbAnimalsRepository.update.
close_reason: Edit-animal sheet (name/species/sex) from the animal detail

title: Edit case intake after creation
description: Add an edit path for the case core (admission reasons, age class, found/admitted dates, find location + geo, intake weight/notes). Today only timeline items are editable; the intake card is read-only. Reuse new_case_screen field widgets in an edit mode.
close_reason: Edit-case-intake sheet (reasons/age/dates/location/weight/notes), carer+supervisor gated

title: Condition code-list editor (CRUD)
description: Supervisor maintains the org's diagnosis vocabulary: add/edit/deactivate conditions (label, is_notifiable, description, active). conditions is supervisor-CRUD by access rule; ConditionsRepository only exposes active() today — wrap create/update/delete.
close_reason: Condition code-list editor: list/add/edit/delete, org-scoped create; admin menu consolidation

title: Org settings screen (contact + retention)
description: Supervisor edits organisations.contact_email/contact_phone and settings JSON (e.g. finder-PII retention window). No PbOrganisationsRepository exists yet — add one + an OrgSettings model over the settings JSON.
close_reason: Org settings screen: name/contact + finder retention; new PbOrganisationsRepository + currentOrganisation provider

title: Member management actions (role, activate/deactivate, remove)
description: On a member: change role (carer/coordinator/supervisor), toggle is_active, remove. Backend users.update/delete already permits supervisors; the onUpdateRequest hook guards escalation. Guard against self-demotion/last-supervisor lockout in UI.
close_reason: Member management sheet: role change, active toggle, remove; self-edit guarded

title: Member list & detail (team roster)
description: Supervisor-only screen listing org members: name, email, role, active/inactive, and un-onboarded state (verified=false / never logged in). Reached from the admin area. Uses PbUsersRepository (add a members() listing beyond activeMembers()).
close_reason: Team roster screen with role + status badges; invite moved into a sheet

title: UX Phase C — Care workflow (status & handoff)
description: Care workflow is thin for a team passing birds around. Settle the case status lifecycle (depends on blp.1 decision) and add manual status transitions; add 'hand this case to another carer' (sets cases.active_carer — share-on-handoff hook already fires on change); add coordinator assign/route.
close_reason: UX Phase C complete: 3-state lifecycle + actions card (C.1), handoff/move split + handoff surfaced (C.2); coordinator routing covered by create+handoff so C.3 closed without a rule change.

title: UX Phase B — Edit & correct (data integrity)
description: Casual carers fat-finger data entry and most core records are write-once. Make them correctable: edit case intake after creation (species, reasons, notes, dates, find location); edit animal identity (name/species/sex); edit & delete dispositions (with case re-open); self-service profile edit (name/phone — access rule already widened).
close_reason: UX Phase B complete: edit case intake, animal identity, dispositions (with re-open), and profile self-edit

title: UX Phase A — Team & org administration (supervisor)
description: Supervisors can invite members but cannot manage them or the org afterwards. Backend (access rules + users.onUpdateRequest guard) and PbUsersRepository CRUD already support it; only the UI is missing. Scope: member list/detail, role change, activate/deactivate, remove; visibility of un-onboarded invites; org settings (contact + retention window in organisations.settings JSON); condition code-list editor (conditions is supervisor-CRUD by rule but UI is read-only).
close_reason: UX Phase A complete: team roster, member management, org settings, condition code-list

title: Weight UI: record from animal detail + life-long trend
description: With weights animal-scoped: record weight from the animal lifetime detail (no case); show a life-long weight trend on the animal detail; the case overview's weight trend filters to that case's weights (or window). When recording inside a case, stamp the case too.
close_reason: Animal detail Weight section: latest reading + record-weight action (no case) + life-long trend. WeightTrendChart.forCase / forAnimal; case overview keeps case-window, animal shows lifetime curve. Widget test; analyze clean, 174 green.

title: Weight: animal-scoped (animal required, case optional) + backfill
description: Make weights animal-level so they can be recorded without a case and tracked across cases (residents). Migration: add required 'animal' relation to weights, make 'case' optional; backfill animal from case for existing rows; update access rules (animal-scoped: visible if you can read the animal/org; or follow case when present). Model: Weight gains animal + nullable caseId. Mirrors markings (animal + optional appliedInCase). Verify on live PB.
close_reason: Weights animal-scoped: migration 1700000020 adds required animal, case optional, backfills, animal-owns-cascade, org-wide access (animal identity layer like markings). Weight model gains animal (caseId nullable); entry sheet takes animalId + optional caseId; weightsForAnimal provider + repo.forAnimal. Verified on live PB (56 checks: org-wide weight, recordable with no case). analyze clean, 173+14 green.

title: 'Open a new case for this animal' shortcut
description: A resident relapse currently needs the blank new-case form + ring re-id search. Add a 'new case' action on the animal lifetime detail (and aviary resident rows) that opens the new-case flow prefilled/linked to this animal.
acceptance_criteria: From an animal/resident the user can start a new case already linked to that animal; analyze clean + test.
close_reason: Animal detail has a 'New case' action -> /cases/new?animal=<id>; NewCaseScreen takes optional animalId and preloads it as the linked animal (skips re-id). Residents reach it via their animal detail. Widget test; analyze clean, 173 green.

title: Case <-> animal navigation link
description: Case detail has no link to the animal lifetime record. Add an animal link (tap the header/avatar or an action) from the case detail to /animals/:id, closing the navigation loop.
acceptance_criteria: From a case detail the user can open the animal lifetime record; analyze clean + test.
close_reason: Case detail now has an 'Open animal record' app-bar action -> /animals/:id, closing the case<->animal nav loop. Avatar stays photo-edit; separate affordance. analyze clean, 172 green.

title: Manage markings from the animal lifetime detail (no case needed)
description: Markings are already animal-level (animal + optional appliedInCase) but showMarkingSheet requires a caseId and the animal detail shows them read-only. Make caseId optional; add add/edit/remove marking actions on the animal lifetime detail (appliedInCase=null when outside a case); invalidate markingsForAnimal. Keep the case-timeline marking view.
acceptance_criteria: From the animal detail a user can add/edit/remove a marking with no case; markings still show in the case timeline; analyze clean + tests.
close_reason: Markings now manageable from the animal lifetime detail: showMarkingSheet caseId optional; animal detail markings section has apply (no case) + per-marking edit/mark-removed/delete; applied_in_case null when outside a case. Case timeline view unchanged. Widget test; analyze clean, 172 green.

title: Animal-centric records — manage data without a case
description: Make the animal the hub for data that belongs to it (markings, weight, photo) so it can be recorded WITHOUT an open case — essential now that aviary residents have no active case. Cases remain only for treatment episodes (conditions, meds, handoffs, disposition). See analysis 2026-06-24.
close_reason: Animal-centric records complete: markings/weight/photo + new-case shortcut all work without an open case from the animal hub; case<->animal navigation closed; weights animal-scoped (migration 1700000020). 5 tasks.

title: Case overview: list prior cases for the animal at the bottom
description: On the case detail Overview tab, append a 'Previous cases' section listing the animal's OTHER cases (exclude the current one), newest-first, when any exist. Reuse caseSummariesForAnimalProvider (FED-7.6) so it shows the full history incl. cases the user can't open — accessible ones tap into case detail, inaccessible render as non-tappable stubs (same treatment as the animal lifetime record). Hide the section entirely when the animal has no other cases. Keep it terse (number + status + admission date).
acceptance_criteria: 1) Overview tab shows a prior-cases list (current case excluded) only when others exist. 2) Accessible cases navigate to case detail; inaccessible show as stubs. 3) Reuses the FED-7.6 summary provider (no new backend). 4) Widget test for present/absent cases; analyze clean.
close_reason: Case Overview now shows an 'Andere Fälle'/'Other cases' card: animal's other cases (current excluded), newest-first, hidden when none. Reuses animalLifetimeProvider (FED-7.6) so inaccessible cases render as non-tappable stubs. Extracted shared CaseSummaryTile (also used by animal lifetime list) showing start–end span. Added ended_at to case_summaries view (migration 1700000018, latest disposition date) + CaseSummary.endedAt. Verified on live PB (50 checks); analyze clean, 150+14 tests green.

title: Header avatar: animal picture w/ fallback to latest case admission photo
description: Add a round avatar to the shared detail header. Source order: (1) an explicit animal picture if set, else (2) the most recent case's first intake photo for that animal, else (3) a placeholder (pets icon). Allow setting/replacing a dedicated animal picture that is SEPARATE from case intake photos. Needs backend: a new 'photo' file field on the animals collection (new migration; animals are org-wide readable so the file inherits that). Model: add Animal.photo + fileUrl access via animals repo. UI: round avatar in the header (tap to view/replace on the animal detail; read-only on the case header). Reuse the existing image_picker injection (imagePickerProvider) and createWithFiles/updateWithFiles + fileUrl(thumb) patterns. Fallback-to-latest-case-photo should reuse case_summaries/cases for the animal.
acceptance_criteria: 1) animals.photo file field via new migration; verified against a running PB stack. 2) Animal model exposes the photo; repo gives a thumb URL. 3) Header shows animal photo, else latest case admission photo, else placeholder, in a round avatar. 4) User can upload/replace the animal picture from the animal detail; it does not alter case intake photos. 5) Tests for the fallback selection logic + analyze clean.
close_reason: Header avatar (round): animal photo -> latest case intake photo -> placeholder, via pure pickAvatarSource + animalAvatarUrl provider + AnimalAvatar widget. Editable (gallery/camera/remove) from both animal & case headers. Backend animals.photo file field (migration 1700000017) verified on live PB (upload+readback rule test, 50 checks). Case header renders avatar from case.animal id so header never briefly centres. analyze clean, 148+14 tests green.

title: Shared case/animal detail header (adopt case-overview style)
description: The case detail header (_Header in case_detail_screen.dart) and the animal lifetime header (_Identity in animal_detail_screen.dart) are separate implementations that diverge and duplicate layout. Extract ONE shared header widget and use it in both. Adopt the case-overview style as the canonical look (name-first headline, species/subtitle line, status chip). The case header shows case status (CaseStatus); the animal header shows lifetime status (LifetimeStatus) — parameterise the chip/subtitle so one widget serves both. Prefer a widget in lib/ui or features/cases shared spot.
acceptance_criteria: 1) A single header widget renders both case detail and animal detail. 2) Visual style matches today's case-overview header. 3) Status chip is correct per screen (case vs lifetime status). 4) No regression in existing case/animal detail widget tests; analyze clean.
close_reason: Extracted DetailHeader (lib/ui/widgets/detail_header.dart): title + optional subtitle + optional status chip + optional leading slot (for ctw.7). Case _Header and animal _Identity both delegate to it; animal screen dropped its Card and centres the header block but keeps identical case-overview internals. 3 widget tests; analyze clean, 145 tests green.

title: Decide case status lifecycle — are in_treatment/rehab/ready_for_release needed?
description: The cases.status enum (migration 1700000004_cases.js) defines in_care | in_treatment | rehab | ready_for_release | disposed, but only two values are ever set, by hooks in pb_hooks/main.pb.js: in_care (default on case create) and disposed (on a terminal disposition). The three middle stages are unreachable — no app UI and no hook transitions a case into them. The app only reads status (list, detail chip, dashboard). REQUIREMENTS.md:164 calls status 'derived/maintained'; the dashboard (REQUIREMENTS.md:289 / FED-7.1) breaks down 'cases by status', so today it would always show in_care=N and the other active stages 0.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Decide whether the intermediate stages (in_treatment/rehab/ready_for_release) are needed at all, or whether in_care + disposed suffices. 2) If KEPT: add a transition mechanism (manual status picker on case detail and/or derive from clinical state, e.g. ready_for_release when is_releasable, in_treatment when active meds). 3) If DROPPED: new migration removing them from the enum, plus remove labels (cases_labels.dart + l10n) and the dashboard breakdown rows. 4) Revisit the FED-7.1 dashboard breakdown either way (note: empty rows were hidden as an interim fix).
close_reason: Decided: 3-state lifecycle in_care -> ready_for_release -> disposed. Drop in_treatment/rehab (treatment detail lives in the timeline). ready_for_release is a manual flag; disposed set by disposition hook.

title: FED-7.6 — Animal lifetime detail (all cases)
description: One animal's full lifetime record: identity (name/species/sex), markings (active + historic), lifetime status, and ALL its cases (current + historic) newest-first. Reuse casesForAnimal + markingsForAnimal. Inaccessible cases (held by another carer, not shared) render as a non-tappable stub showing case number + status + dates only (no clinical detail); accessible cases open into case detail. Reachable from the Animals registry and from a case (animal link).
close_reason: Animal lifetime detail: identity (name/species/sex + lifetime-status chip), all markings (active+historic), every case newest-first. Accessible cases tap into case detail; inaccessible ones render as non-tappable stubs (number/status/dates + no-access badge) sourced from a new org-wide case_summaries PB view (migration 1700000016) that projects only non-clinical columns. New CaseSummary model + repo + animalLifetime provider. Verified on live PB: 5 new rule tests (48 total) prove summary readable org-wide, no clinical leak, org isolation. Flutter mapper+widget tests green.

title: FED-7.5 — Animals registry (list + search)
description: Searchable list of the org's animals (persistent identities, org-wide readable). Row shows name, species, active marking code(s), lifetime status (in_care / at_large_released / in_aviary / deceased). Search by name or ring/chip code (reuse markings.activeByCode + animals.searchByName). Carers still see their animals here. Tap -> animal lifetime detail. Keep aviary residency in mind for later (Phase 6) but do not build aviary features now.
close_reason: Animals registry: searchable list of org animals (org-wide readable). Row shows name, species, active marking codes, lifetime status. Search matches name or active ring/chip code. Joins animals+active markings in one fetch; pure filterAnimals() unit-tested + widget tests. Rows tap to animal lifetime detail via new /animals/:id route (placeholder screen now, body filled by FED-7.6). Added lifetimeStatusLabel + l10n. Unblocks FED-7.6.

title: FED-7.4 — All-cases browser (filters + search)
description: Browse all cases the user can access (server rules scope it: carer = own + shared; coordinator/supervisor = all in org). DEFAULT VIEW = the user's own/active cases ('My cases'); an 'All' toggle/filter widens to everything they can access. Filters: status (incl. disposed/historic), active-vs-closed, species, date range; plus text search (case number / animal name). Tap-through to case detail. Access-scoped, not a new permission.
close_reason: All-cases browser: scope toggle (mine/all, server-scoped), activity (active/closed/all), species, admission date-range filters + text search (case no / animal name). Compact UI — search always visible, secondary filters in a badged filter bottom-sheet that updates live. Pure filterCases() over one client-side fetch (cases+animals); unit + widget tests. Replaced dead myCasesProvider; writes now refresh casesBrowserData+dashboardSummary. Dashboard->browser deep-link pre-filter filed as follow-up.

title: FED-7.0 — App navigation shell (adaptive)
description: Replace the single 'My cases' home with an adaptive top-level navigation: Material NavigationBar (bottom) on narrow screens and NavigationRail on wide/web. Destinations: Dashboard, Cases, Animals. Profile + supervisor Admin stay reachable from the app bar. Prerequisite for the rest of Phase 7.
close_reason: Adaptive nav shell: StatefulShellRoute (Dashboard/Cases/Animals) with NavigationBar<600w / NavigationRail>=600w (extended>=840). Profile+admin via shared AccountActions in each tab app bar. HomeScreen split into CasesScreen + Dashboard/Animals placeholders. Tests + analyze green.

title: Semantic medication frequency (reminder-ready)
description: Replace free-text medication frequency with a structured interval model so reminders can be built later (requirements: keep dates structured). Add frequency_kind (once|scheduled|as_needed) + interval_hours to medications; prescription form uses interval presets (q24/q12/q8/q6/q48/custom) + once/PRN; tile shows a derived label. Follow-up to FED-4.6.
close_reason: Medication frequency is now structured + reminder-ready: medications gains frequency_kind (once|scheduled|as_needed) + interval_hours (migration). Prescription form uses interval presets (once/q24/q12/q8/q6/q48/custom/PRN) mapping to (kind, hours); tile shows a derived label (e.g. 'Twice daily', 'Every 10 h'). Free-text frequency kept as an optional note. A reminder = last administration + interval_hours. Tests for default+chosen preset and tile label.

title: Tidy case detail into Overview/History tabs
description: Case detail was growing too long (header + intake + chart + timeline stacked). Split into a persistent name-first header over two tabs: Overview (intake summary + weight trend) and History (the unified chronology). User-chosen layout.
close_reason: Case detail is now a persistent name-first header over two tabs: Overview (weight trend + intake summary) and History (the unified CaseTimeline, title suppressed since the tab names it). Keeps the screen short as more record kinds land. Detail test updated to switch tabs.

Events interleave newest-first; future record kinds slot in. Replaces the separate JournalSection/_TimelineSection. Tests for ordering + empty state.

Added updateWithFiles() (multipart, keep-list pattern) to the repo; generalized the entry sheet to create-or-edit; per-entry overflow menu for edit/delete. Tests for edit (drops removed attachment), delete, and updateWithFiles.

title: Password-reset email: action URL + appURL (SMTP delivery done)
description: SMTP DELIVERY is done: pb_hooks/smtp.pb.js applies FEDERFALL_SMTP_* env (host/port/user/pass/tls/sender) into PB settings on boot (PB's recommended onBootstrap pattern, discussion #1551); /api/federfall/info reports passwordReset:true when configured. REMAINING for invites/reset to actually work end to end: (1) set meta.appURL from env (e.g. FEDERFALL_APP_URL) so mail links resolve to the operator's domain; (2) point the users collection 'forgot password' email template action URL at the app reset route, format {APP_URL}/auth/confirm-reset?token={TOKEN} (ConfirmResetScreen reads ?token=) — bake via migration so it's reproducible. Belongs with the self-host deployment guide, not the M1 deploy gate.
close_reason: Closed

title: Bootstrap first supervisor (doc + optional dev seed)
description: No users row is seeded (FED-1.1 seeds only the single organisations row), and registration is invite-only (FED-3.2). So the FIRST supervisor must be created out-of-band — chicken-and-egg for the invite flow and the M1 deploy. PocketBase superusers (admin UI) are NOT app-level Supervisors. Deliver: (1) a documented manual runbook step — create the first 'users' record via the PB admin UI with role=supervisor, is_active=true, org=<seeded org id>; (2) OPTIONAL dev-only guarded seed/script that upserts a local dev supervisor for testing (never run in prod). Currently only exists ad-hoc in local pb_data. Prereq for FED-3.2 (invite needs a supervisor) and required by FED-3.5 (deploy).
notes: Self-host reframe: this is a per-operator FIRST-RUN step (every operator bootstraps their own first supervisor), documented in the deployment guide. The optional dev seed stays dev-only.
close_reason: Closed

title: Generalize offline read cache to all collections
description: Extend FED-2.6 beyond recently-viewed cases: a generic RecordCache baked into PbRepository so every repository's getOne/list reads are cached and served offline on network failure, with writes updating/invalidating the cache. Replaces the cases-only decorator.
close_reason: Offline read cache generalized to ALL collections (not just cases). Pluggable RecordCache interface baked into PbRepository: every getOne/list caches raw records and is served from cache on network failure; writes refresh/evict. NoopRecordCache default keeps it opt-in. App provides bounded shared_preferences-backed PrefsRecordCache wired into all 14 repo providers. 13 cache/repo tests (7 data + 6 app).

Set up flutter_launcher_icons + flutter_native_splash (per-flavor if desired) and generate android/ios/web assets. Deferred from FED-0.3 at user request.

Prescription sheet (drug/dose/route/frequency/dates/controlled/instructions/prescriber) and dose sheet (prefill+link from a plan, or ad-hoc; administered_by=carer). title: FED-8.6 — Operator ops tooling: backup/restore, monitoring, geocoding
description: Give self-host OPERATORS the tools + docs to run their own instance safely (not run ours): (1) Backup/restore — ship a documented procedure + helper script for the pb_data volume (PB SQLite); the real deliverable is a TESTED restore that proves a backup round-trips. (2) Monitoring — document the existing container healthcheck and how to wire external uptime/alerting; we don't host monitoring. (3) Geocoding — document the public-OSM Nominatim usage policy (real-contact UA, rate limits) AND provide a self-host option (env-configurable FEDERFALL_NOMINATIM_URL + map tiles). All config via env in compose. Feeds the self-host deployment guide.
notes: Resolved by decision: PocketBase's built-in backups are the backup/restore story — no custom script. Operator ops are already documented in docs/DEPLOYMENT.md: PB built-in backups + 'restore at least once', the container healthcheck at /api/health for external uptime monitoring, and the geocoding caveat with self-host env knobs (FEDERFALL_NOMINATIM_URL etc.). Nothing further to build.
close_reason: Closed

title: FED-8.2 — GDPR export/delete
description: Data export + delete for a person on request.
notes: Decision: not building dedicated export/erase tooling. GDPR data-subject requests (access/portability + erasure) are handled manually by a supervisor/admin via the PocketBase admin dashboard — read out the person's records to satisfy an access request, and blank the identifying fields (anonymise in place, keeping case history) to satisfy erasure. The finder-retention job (federfall-69p) already auto-anonymises finders after the retention window. Revisit if request volume makes the manual path impractical.
close_reason: Closed

title: Protected thumbnails: token-stripped cacheKey to stop re-downloads
description: Follow-up to 49l.1. Protected image URLs now carry a short-lived ?token= (~2min). Fix: adopt cached_network_image (or a custom ImageProvider) with a cacheKey that strips the token query param, so the same file caches across token rotations. Affects case intake photos, journal attachments, animal avatars. Low priority — purely an efficiency/UX nicety, not a correctness/security issue.
close_reason: Implemented via cached_network_image with a token-stripped cacheKey (CachedFileImage + fileCacheKey in lib/ui/widgets/cached_file_image.dart). CachedNetworkImageProvider keys its in-memory ImageCache on cacheKey??url, so a rotated ~90s file token resolves to the same entry (no re-decode/re-fetch); on mobile flutter_cache_manager keys disk on cacheKey too and honors PB's Cache-Control (max-age=30d, verified via live probe). Honors server caching rather than forcing per-open revalidation, per product decision. Applied to intake photos, journal attachments, animal avatar, full-screen viewer. Tests: cached_file_image_test + updated image_viewer_test.

comments.author: Jan-Henrik Bruhn
comments.text:
DESIGN (agreed 2026-06-25) — relational, follows case_conditions child pattern. Optional & skippable; intake never blocked. Repeatable timeline record (intake exam + later re-exams), one showExamSheet() + tile + sealed _Event.

Two collections:

exams (one row/exam):
  case (rel), animal (rel, denormalized like weights), examined_at (date, def now),
  examiner (rel user), body_condition (int 1-5; 1=emaciated 3=ideal 5=obese, nullable),
  hydration (enum: normal/mild/moderate/severe, nullable),
  mentation (enum: bright/quiet/depressed/unresponsive = BAR/QAR/.., nullable),
  notes (text), org.

exam_findings (child, one row per ASSESSED system — sparse):
  exam (rel), system (enum BodySystem), status (enum FindingStatus: normal/abnormal),
  note (text), org.
  Fetched in ONE query per case (filter exam.case=X), grouped client-side under each exam
  (same concurrent timeline-source pattern as worklist). No N+1.

Enums in enums.dart with wire values: Hydration, Mentation, BodySystem, FindingStatus
(body_condition stays plain int). Labels via cases_labels.dart, German primary.

UI sheet = 3 zones: (1) vitals always visible — BCS segmented 1-5, hydration & mentation
chips, nothing preselected; (2) "Befunde nach Körperregion" collapsed, each system o.B./
auffällig tri-state via untouched, note field appears only on auffällig; (3) free notes.
Intake entry = optional collapsed expander reusing same widget body; weight shown read-only
(stays its own kind/trend), not re-entered.

Rejected: JSON findings blob (untyped, unqueryable, breaks fromRecord/wire-enum convention);
fixed per-system columns (migration churn per system, mostly-null cols).

Defer to later: per-system numeric subscores (pododermatitis 1-5, 9-pt keel), photo on a
finding, cross-exam by-system reporting.

title: Rewrite repository layer to use $PocketBase RequestPolicy.cacheAndNetwork
description:
All ~20 repository classes in packages/federfall_data/lib/src/repositories/ need to be reimplemented on top of $PocketBase instead of PbRepository.

Pattern for each repository:
- Keep Repository<T> interface unchanged
- Implement methods using $PocketBase.collection(name).{getOne,getList,create,update,delete}()
- Use requestPolicy: RequestPolicy.cacheAndNetwork (the offline-first default)
- Convert RecordModel → domain model via existing Case.fromRecord / Animal.fromRecord etc.
- Drop PrefsRecordCache usage entirely

Priority order: cases, animals, case_conditions, treatments, weights (the core daily-use data) first; then worklist_entries, findings, exams, and remaining collections.

title: Export PocketBase schema and add pb_schema.json as a bundled Flutter asset
description:
pocketbase_drift requires the collection schema to be exported from PocketBase Admin UI (Settings → Export collections → JSON) and bundled as a Flutter asset.

- Export the current schema from the running PocketBase instance
- Save as apps/federfall/assets/pb_schema.json
- Add assets/pb_schema.json to flutter: assets: in pubspec.yaml
- Wire into app bootstrap: await client.db.setSchema(schema)
- Add schema re-export step to the migration/runbook docs

NOTE: The schema must be re-exported after every PocketBase collection migration. Document this as part of the deployment runbook.
